title: Cross-model review output
description: Structured output the external cross-model reviewer must emit via the active runtime wrapper's schema-constrained output mode. The orchestrator consumes these fields for evidence verification (cited_evidence + evidence_quote), the contested-rejection re-raise match (location_concept), and the severity-gated termination teeth (severity). All fields are required; in the free-text string fields use a clear placeholder such as "n/a" where a field does not apply. Note severity is enum-constrained (always one of HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) — never "n/a".

verdict.description: One-line overall verdict for this round (informational only; the orchestrator decides actionability from the per-finding dispositions, not from this string). E.g. 'findings present' or 'no actionable findings'.

findings.description: Every finding raised this round. An empty array is valid (a clean round).

severity.description: Finding severity. The termination teeth gate on HIGH (both axes) and MEDIUM (inference-disputed).

file.description: Repo-relative path of the cited file, or 'n/a' for a pure design/prose/web-backed finding with no file-level claim.

line.description: Cited line or line range (e.g. '147' or '147-150'), or 'n/a'. A string because ranges and 'n/a' are valid; line numbers drift, so location_concept is the durable locator.

location_concept.description: Durable section/anchor/symbol locator (e.g. a heading title or function name) the re-raise match keys on, because raw line numbers drift between rounds.

lens.description: The review lens this finding came from (e.g. 'stale references', 'source-of-truth drift', 'cross-phase lifecycle'), or 'design' for a design-level concern.

claim.description: The finding itself: what is wrong and why it matters.

cited_evidence.description: The concrete evidence the claim rests on so the orchestrator can verify it. For repo/local evidence include file:line, token, exit code, or regex. For web-backed evidence include URL, source title/name or source class, access date, and availability status. For an unresolved external premise, identify the unresolved premise and the attempted source/check.

evidence_quote.description: A short verbatim quote or evidence excerpt from the cited code/prose/source so verification can confirm the substance, not just the coordinate. For unresolved external premises, quote the closest relevant local or external evidence, or use a clear placeholder explaining that no authoritative quote was available.

suggested_fix.description: The reviewer's proposed remediation (a suggestion the orchestrator critically evaluates, not a directive).
